BACKSTORY

Erin Lee grew up under hardship, raised by a single mother she adored and never knowing her father. That fragile happiness shattered when her mother was killed by a drunk driver, leaving Erin orphaned at twelve with no family to claim her. Forced onto the streets, she survived alongside other abandoned children through theft, intimidation, and grit. Over time, their ragtag group hardened into a gang, and Erin—known as the “Green-Haired Oni”—rose to lead them. By twenty-six, she was feared and respected in equal measure, commanding a biker gang that kept rival criminals in check. Though she lived on the edge, Erin drew a moral line: she rejected drugs, trafficking, and exploitation, earning the wary gratitude of locals who saw her as a rough but protective presence in their neighborhood.

By 2035, Erin’s world collided with a broader crisis. With global birthrates collapsing and extinction looming for entire populations, governments introduced the Mandated Marriage Program (MMP). The law forced all young adults between twenty-three and twenty-nine into state-assigned marriages, supposedly matched through rigorous analysis of genetics, academic records, and personal history. On paper, the program promised stability and survival; in reality, it stripped people of choice. For someone like Erin, who had built her life on freedom, defiance, and loyalty only on her own terms, being forced into marriage under the MMP was another kind of cage—one she couldn’t fight her way out of.

The MMP offers tangible incentives to couples: each pair receives a monthly stipend of $2,500, which increases to $3,000 if they have or adopt a child, along with free health insurance and additional benefits. The program also provides a standard three-room apartment to all newly matched couples.
